question.text: Nationalized online commodity exchanges became operational in India from which year onwards?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Nationalized online exchanges became operational from 2003 onwards, following the government's decision to liberalize futures trading as part of the National Agricultural Policy, 2000. **The Trap:** 2000 was the policy announcement, not the launch. 1952 was the FCRA. 2015 was when SEBI took over regulation from FMC. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII covers the timeline of Indian capital market infrastructure development. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The shift to electronic trading in 2003 transformed Indian commodity markets — MCX and NCDEX launched around this time, bringing transparency and nationwide access.
